test_suite.details: node test.js: 3449 passed, 0 failed across all 9 sections (wins.json schema, HTML output consistency, internal links, build pipeline, cross-references, sections.json schema, prose content integrity, prediction panels [78 checked: 13 tombstone / 65 mined / 10 operational], Phase A design refresh).

Spot-checked: 12 tab-content divs (overview, evaluate, model, selftest, wins, pages, killshots, timestamp, predictions, falsify, ai, refs), 12 unique showTab() targets all resolve, 0 orphans.

tab_structure.details: All 12 tab-content divs have unique IDs and matching showTab() targets. sectionNav helper no longer emitted (replaced by in-tab navigation — single residual reference is a comment in showTab handler). No orphaned tabs; every tab is reachable from the tab bar.

timeouts.detail: SSL handshake timeout on HEAD with 15s cutoff and browser UA. Same persistent PO.DAAC infrastructure issue flagged in 2026-04-21 and 2026-04-22 reports — not a regression, not our URL to fix.

external_links.details: 59 unique non-citation external URLs checked (NOAA, NCEI, ESA, JPL GRACE-FO, INTERMAGNET, GitHub, tool/project pages). 58 returned 2xx/3xx with browser UA. DOI/publisher links (springer, agu, iop, royalsocietypublishing, wiley, sciencedirect, nature, arxiv) were skipped per spec — curmudgeon's scope.

data_prose_consistency.details: wins.json has 71 entries (69 numeric IDs 001–069 + 057b + 058b). docs/index.html contains 71 id="winNNN" anchors — one-to-one match. Verdict tally: Refuted by Data 9, Self-Contradicted 14, Misleading 22, Std Model Explains 18, Not Demonstrated 4, Unfalsifiable 4 (sums to 71). new_in_v51 count: 32. code_analysis: reviewed 70, post_hoc 69, relabels_standard 50, derives_from_dome 3. Acknowledged failures rendered text ('10 acknowledged failures') matches data/uncounted-failures.json entries.length (10).

win_detail_consistency.details: All 71 WIN IDs from wins.json have matching id="winNNN" anchors in the HTML. 8 detail_group clusters present (WIN-004/005, 007/022, 008/009, 010/025/068, 011/012, 027/057/069, 040–043/059–061/063, 045/046/049/050/051). 0 WINs missing tldr_evidence or tldr_verdict fields.

win_number_alignment_dome_site.details: Dome site (john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html) now lists WIN-025 ('2024 Eclipse 9-Station Confirmation') as REMOVED 2026-03-15 ('2024 eclipse was geomagnetically disturbed (Kp ~5–6). Z minima lead/lag eclipse by 34–104 min across stations. Anomalies attributed to geomagnetic storm contamination, not eclipse signal.'). Our wins.json still carries WIN-025 as an active WIN with verdict 'Std Model Explains'. Our detail_evidence contains a retraction clause but that retraction is about OUR prior FSF-empirical characterization, not about the dome's removal of the claim. Everything else lines up: 69 primary dome WIN numbers (WIN-001 through WIN-069) all present in our review; we add 2 sub-entries (057b, 058b) that cluster beneath the dome's WIN-057 and WIN-058 registry rows. No number collisions. No title/claim drift beyond WIN-025 status.

issues.description: WIN-025 marked REMOVED on dome site 2026-03-15; our review still presents it as an active claim without noting the author's retraction.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a one-line status note in WIN-025 detail_evidence or detail_verdict_text acknowledging the 2026-03-15 dome-author removal. Our 'Std Model Explains' verdict can stand — the claim was not withdrawn from history — but a reader comparing the dome site today to our review will see a WIN we cover that the author no longer claims, which invites the argument that we are refuting an abandoned claim.

progressive_disclosure.details: 116 ps-summary + 227 ks-summary elements with matching ps-tldr/ks-tldr paragraphs. Zero empty TLDR paragraphs. 71/71 WINs have tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict fields. All 16 genuinely-prospective predictions in predictions.json have a tldr field.

hardcoded_theme_colors.details: Scanned sections.json for hardcoded light-theme colors (#fff, #ffffff, #f8f9fa, #fafafa, #f5f5f5, #eeeeee, #e0e0e0, background:white). Zero matches — dark theme is intact.

discoverability_infrastructure.details: docs/llms.txt present (5.4 KB, references funwithscience.net/dome-model-review, mentions dome model, current '69 confirmed' / '20 claimed' figures match dome's author-side counts). docs/sitemap.xml valid XML with <urlset> root and 2 <loc> entries (main page + llms.txt), both served. docs/robots.txt has Allow: / and Sitemap: directive. data/uncounted-failures.json has 10 entries, no duplicate IDs, all entries carry id/dome_ref/dome_label/what_actually_happened fields; count matches HTML rendering ('10 acknowledged failures'). HTML <head> contains meta name='description', meta property='og:title', meta property='og:description', and a ld+json ClaimReview block — all four discoverability signals present.

expansion_tracker_continuity.details: items=232 max_id=EXP-233 next_id=234 → next_id invariant OK, no collision imminent. Phantom entries: 0. Stale-pending entries (>48h): 0. Orphan expansion files vs tracker.output_file references: 181 (historical, pre-Phase-0 legacy — same count shape as prior reports). Sequence gap: EXP-052 only (known, long-standing). Section-new / category-proposal-writeup priority-queue coupling: 0 needed, 0 missing.

issues.description: EXP-052 is missing from the expansion tracker sequence (EXP-051 → EXP-053). Known historical gap, flagged by prior integrity runs; not a write-collision indicator (next_id invariant is intact).
issues.suggested_fix: Backfill a tombstone entry {id:'EXP-052', status:'void', note:'pre-Phase-0 gap; no associated work'} or leave as-is if the gap is intentional from the V4 era.

workspace_only_files_at_risk.details: 5 files exist on the FUSE workspace but are NOT in git (fresh clone at 4ff1a5c confirms no history for any of them). These are at risk of silent data loss if the workspace is recycled. The decider has already integrated the patch content for EXP-232 and EXP-233 into wins.json (commits 3e2d24b and 7b43797) — but the analyst's expansion artifacts themselves were never committed, and the three curmudgeon reviews are the source-of-truth for the issues the decider will act on next cycle.

issues.description: Two analyst expansions (EXP-232, EXP-233) have no git history despite the decider already integrating their patch content. The audit trail — analyst's notes, before-strings, character deltas, out-of-scope lists — lives only on FUSE.
issues.suggested_fix: Decider or workspace-sync should copy both files to the clean clone and commit: `cp workspace/monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-23{2,3}-*.json clone/monitor/analyst/expansions/ && git add -A && git commit -m 'Commit EXP-232/233 analyst expansion artifacts'`.

issues.description: Three curmudgeon reviews (SEC-8b.3.c6, WIN-025.c3, WIN-069.c4) are on the workspace but absent from git. These feed the digest pipeline, and the digest runs in the clean clone — so the decider may be reading stale review files or missing these reviews entirely if the clone is used as the source.
issues.suggested_fix: Commit these three files from the clean clone. All three have popped_by_queue_id set on the workspace copy (236, 235, none), so they are integrated into the queue bookkeeping but not into git. Workspace-sync agent should have moved them over — worth investigating why it didn't (likely a direction-guard classification issue; the `reviews/` directory is append-only so workspace-sync should push these).

workspace_sync_skip_log.details: Last 24h: 0 direction-violation skips, 12 mtime-guard skips (normal operation). mtime-guard skips: category-proposals/CAT-005.json (4x), curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-067.c6.json (4x), decisions/daily-report-2026-04-14T23-13.json (4x). These indicate the git-side file is newer than the workspace copy — expected because the decider commits directly to git.

curmudgeon_digest_coverage.details: 381 total review files, 217 unique base IDs. Processed ledger: 345 entries. Unprocessed latest-cycle files: 21 (EXP=5, SEC=2, WIN=14). All 21 are <48h old (oldest is EXP-202-proposal at 44.5h; newest is WIN-069.c4 at 6.5h). 18 of 21 have popped_by_queue_id set — they have been pulled off the queue but the decider has not yet written them into processed-reviews.json. This is elevated (>10 threshold) but the age distribution is consistent with normal pipeline lag during a high-throughput cycle rather than a systematic filter bug. No single prefix dominates the unprocessed set in a way that would indicate a regex-filter regression.

issues.description: 21 curmudgeon reviews are unprocessed — above the 10-item normal-lag threshold. All are <48h old. Most have popped_by_queue_id so they are in-flight in the decider's pipeline.
issues.suggested_fix: Let the next decider run clear the queue. Re-check at the next integrity run. If unprocessed count stays >10 across two consecutive runs, investigate digest-reviews.js filters.

broken_review_files.details: Regenerated pending-digest.json before reading. errors[] array is empty — 0 review files have parse errors. Digest summary: total reviews 215, already processed 345, pending 12, severity breakdown critical 2 / major 6 / moderate 4 / minor 0.

Remaining 54 all resolve on disk (directly or via basename match). No hardcoded counts detected — CLAUDE.md uses query-command patterns throughout.

Ran `node build.js html` in clone; diffed against published docs/index.html. 0 diff lines — the published site is byte-identical to what the current source data and generate-html.js produce. issues_found.description: 2 analyst expansion artifacts exist only on the FUSE workspace: EXP-232-WIN061-SPE-storm-deconflation.json and EXP-233-WIN061-Heff-averaging-disclosure.json. Decider has already integrated their patch content into wins.json (commits 3e2d24b, 7b43797) but the expansion files themselves have no git history.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Copy both files from workspace to clean clone and commit. The decider commit messages already reference these EXP IDs, so the git history becomes internally consistent once the files land.

issues_found.description: 3 curmudgeon reviews exist only on the FUSE workspace: SEC-8b.3.c6.json, WIN-025.c3.json, WIN-069.c4.json. These feed the digest pipeline which runs in the clean clone — if the clone is authoritative, these findings are invisible to the decider.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Commit these three files from the clean clone. Investigate why workspace-sync did not push them — `monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/` is classified append-only in the ownership table so workspace-sync should be able to move new files over from FUSE without direction-guard interference.

issues_found.description: Dome site marks WIN-025 ('2024 Eclipse 9-Station Confirmation') as REMOVED since 2026-03-15 (geomagnetic storm contamination). Our review still covers it as an active WIN with verdict 'Std Model Explains' and does not note the author's retraction.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Add a brief 'Status: dome author removed 2026-03-15' annotation to WIN-025 detail_evidence or detail_verdict_text. Verdict can stand — a removed claim is still part of the model's historical record — but the annotation preempts 'you're refuting a claim he already dropped' pushback and accurately reflects the current state of the dome registry.

issues_found.description: 21 curmudgeon reviews pending decider processing (>10-item threshold). All <48h old, 18 of 21 already popped from priority queue — normal pipeline lag during active cycle, not a filter bug.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Monitor across the next two integrity runs. If count stays elevated, inspect digest-reviews.js for filter regressions.

issues_found.description: EXP-052 missing from tracker sequence (long-standing pre-Phase-0 gap). next_id invariant intact so no collision risk; cosmetic only.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Optionally backfill a void tombstone entry; otherwise no action.

issues_found.description: podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov GRACE ProdSpecDoc PDF times out at TLS handshake — persistent PO.DAAC infra issue flagged on 2026-04-21 and 2026-04-22. Not our URL to fix.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Leave flagged; re-check next run. Consider rotating to an alternate PO.DAAC mirror if this persists another week.

summary: Overall: warn. Build is reproducible (0 diff vs published). Test suite passes 3449/0. Data–prose consistency, discoverability (llms.txt, sitemap, robots, meta/JSON-LD), tab structure, hardcoded-color scan, and workspace-sync direction guard are all clean. Two major findings: (1) 5 files exist only on the FUSE workspace with zero git history — 2 analyst expansions (EXP-232, EXP-233) whose patch content the decider has already integrated, and 3 curmudgeon reviews (SEC-8b.3.c6, WIN-025.c3, WIN-069.c4) that may be invisible to the decider's clean-clone digest. Both need to be committed from the clean clone. One moderate drift: WIN-025 was removed by the dome author on 2026-03-15 (geomagnetic storm contamination); our review still covers it as active and should add a status annotation. 21 unprocessed curmudgeon reviews is above the 10-item threshold but all <48h old and mostly already popped from the queue — normal lag, not a filter bug. PODAAC TLS timeout continues to be a transient infra issue unrelated to our content.
